focus: Slice 3: causal graph + recursive CTE layer

notes: Recursive CTE with bidirectional UNION seed + UNION ALL recursive step traversing causal_edges up to MAX_HOPS(2). Turso lacks WITH RECURSIVE; must be rewritten as iterative app-side loop or stored procedure equivalent.
Recursive CTE for transitive dependency closure. Turso lacks WITH RECURSIVE; collectDependents() and wouldCreateCycle() both depend on this. Must rewrite as iterative app-side BFS.

Core graph table with dynamic optional columns detected via PRAGMA table_info. Migration must preserve the evolving column set; Turso supports PRAGMA table_info but column detection pattern should be verified.
PRAGMA table_info used 3x (lines 382, 562) for dynamic column detection during upsert/bulk insert. Turso supports PRAGMA but behavior may differ; verify column name casing and result shape.
Direct sqlite_master query for table/index existence checks. Turso may expose schema differently; verify sqlite_master availability or substitute with PRAGMA-based detection.
database.exec() runs multi-statement DDL; Turso lacks loadExtension and exec() multi-statement batching may behave differently.
Recursive CTE for dependency traversal; Turso lacks WITH RECURSIVE — must be rewritten as iterative application-side loop.
Explicit better-sqlite3 synchronous transaction wrapper; Turso transactions are async (await tx()). Entire function must become async.
Nested transaction (calls insertEdge which also wraps in transaction); better-sqlite3 supports savepoints but Turso transaction API differs.
PRAGMA table_info() inside transaction for dynamic column discovery; Turso PRAGMA support is limited — may need schema caching.
Synchronous transaction wrapping read-modify-write for atomicity; must become async for Turso.
Multi-statement transaction with fallback SELECT chain; synchronous transaction wrapper blocks Turso migration.
Uses runInTransaction wrapper (from transaction-manager.ts) — that wrapper must become async for Turso.
Multi-statement DDL via database.exec(); Turso exec() may not support multi-statement batching — must split.
Core tombstone lifecycle: read edges → insert tombstones → delete edges in single transaction. Uses runInTransaction which must become async.
Orchestrates multiple write calls (sweep + insert) without wrapping in own transaction; relies on per-call transactionality.
Reads stale edges then delegates to sweep for tombstoning+deletion; two-phase read-then-write pattern needs atomicity guarantee.
Core recursive CTE for graph traversal with bidirectional edges and relation-weighted scoring. Turso lacks WITH RECURSIVE — must rewrite as iterative BFS in application code.
Recursive CTE for graph traversal (2-hop). Turso lacks WITH RECURSIVE — must be replaced with iterative application-side traversal or bounded UNION ALL.
better-sqlite3 Statement type cast used for .all()/.get() — Turso driver has different statement API (async, no .raw()/.pluck()/.iterate()).
Recursive CTE for transitive dependency closure (cycle detection + invalidation). Turso lacks WITH RECURSIVE — must rewrite as iterative BFS.
PRAGMA table_info for dynamic column introspection. Turso/libSQL lacks .pragma() API and PRAGMA results may differ — used at 3 sites in this file.
Fourth PRAGMA table_info site in frontmatter promoter. Same Turso introspection gap — pattern is pervasive across causal layer.
Module-level db reference via better-sqlite3 Database type. Turso migration requires replacing Database.Database with libSQL client type across all causal modules.
